audit_status.source_base: Greek Gospel text tradition, draft aligned to public-domain comparison layer later
audit_status.review_note: Compact complete draft reviewed for passion plot, Bethany anointing, Judas, Passover meal, covenant blood, Gethsemane, arrest, trial, Son of Humanity, Peter denial.

verses.restored: When Jesus finished all these words, he said to his disciples,
You know that after two days the Passover happens, and the Son of Humanity is handed over to be crucified.
Then the chief priests and elders of the people gathered in the courtyard of the high priest called Caiaphas,
and took counsel together to seize Jesus by deceit and kill him.
But they said, Not during the feast, lest an uproar happen among the people.
When Jesus was in Bethany, in the house of Simon the leper,
a woman came to him with an alabaster jar of very costly ointment, and she poured it on his head as he reclined.
Seeing this, the disciples were indignant, saying, Why this waste?
For this could have been sold for much and given to the poor.
Knowing this, Jesus said, Why do you trouble the woman? She has worked a beautiful/good work for me.
For the poor you always have with you, but me you do not always have.
In pouring this ointment on my body, she did it to prepare me for burial.
Amen, I say to you: wherever this good message is proclaimed in the whole world, what she has done will also be spoken in memory of her.
Then one of the twelve, called Judas Iscariot, went to the chief priests
and said, What are you willing to give me, and I will hand him over to you? They weighed out thirty silver pieces to him.
From then he sought an opportune moment to hand him over.
On the first day of Unleavened Bread, the disciples came to Jesus, saying, Where do you want us to prepare for you to eat the Passover?
He said, Go into the city to a certain man and say to him, The Teacher says, My time is near; I will keep the Passover at your house with my disciples.
The disciples did as Jesus instructed them, and they prepared the Passover.
When evening came, he reclined with the twelve.
As they were eating, he said, Amen, I say to you: one of you will hand me over.
They were deeply grieved, and each began to say to him, Surely not I, Lord?
He answered, The one dipping his hand with me in the bowl, this one will hand me over.

The Son of Humanity goes as it has been written concerning him, but woe to that person through whom the Son of Humanity is handed over. It would have been beautiful/good for that person if he had not been born.
Judas, the one handing him over, answered, Surely not I, Rabbi? He said to him, You have said it.
As they were eating, Jesus took bread, blessed, broke it, gave it to the disciples, and said, Take, eat; this is my body.
Taking a cup and giving thanks, he gave it to them, saying, Drink from it, all of you.
For this is my blood of the covenant, poured out for many for release of sins/misalignments.
I say to you: I will not drink from this fruit of the vine from now until that day when I drink it new with you in my Father's reign.
After singing a hymn, they went out to the Mount of Olives.
Then Jesus said to them, You will all stumble because of me this night; for it has been written, I will strike the shepherd, and the sheep of the flock will be scattered.
But after I am raised, I will go before you to Galilee.
Peter answered, Even if all stumble because of you, I will never stumble.
Jesus said to him, Amen, I say to you: this night, before a rooster sounds, you will deny me three times.
Peter said, Even if I must die with you, I will not deny you. All the disciples said likewise.
Then Jesus came with them to a place called Gethsemane and said to the disciples, Sit here while I go there and pray.
Taking Peter and the two sons of Zebedee, he began to be grieved and deeply distressed.
Then he said to them, My soul/life is deeply grieved to the point of death; remain here and keep watch with me.
Going forward a little, he fell on his face, praying, My Father, if possible, let this cup pass from me; nevertheless, not as I will, but as you will.
He came to the disciples and found them sleeping, and said to Peter, So, were you not strong enough to keep watch with me one hour?
Keep watch and pray, so that you may not enter testing. The spirit is willing, but the flesh is weak.
Again, a second time, he went and prayed, My Father, if this cannot pass unless I drink it, let your will happen.
Again he came and found them sleeping, for their eyes were heavy.
Leaving them again, he went away and prayed a third time, saying the same word again.
Then he came to the disciples and said, Sleep the rest and rest? Look, the hour has drawn near, and the Son of Humanity is handed over into sinners' hands.
Rise, let us go. Look, the one handing me over has drawn near.
While he was still speaking, look, Judas, one of the twelve, came, and with him a great crowd with swords and clubs from the chief priests and elders of the people.
The one handing him over had given them a sign, saying, Whomever I kiss, he is the one; seize him.
Immediately he came to Jesus and said, Greetings, Rabbi, and kissed him.
Jesus said to him, Friend, do what you came for. Then they came and laid hands on Jesus and seized him.
And look, one of those with Jesus stretched out his hand, drew his sword, struck the servant of the high priest, and cut off his ear.
Then Jesus said to him, Return your sword to its place, for all who take the sword will perish by the sword.
Or do you think I cannot call on my Father, and he will now place beside me more than twelve legions of messengers?
How then would the Scriptures be fulfilled, that it must happen this way?
In that hour Jesus said to the crowds, Have you come out as against a robber, with swords and clubs to arrest me? Day by day I sat teaching in the temple, and you did not seize me.
All this happened so that the writings of the prophets might be fulfilled. Then all the disciples left him and fled.
Those who seized Jesus led him away to Caiaphas the high priest, where the scribes and elders were gathered.
Peter followed him from far away to the courtyard of the high priest, entered, and sat with the attendants to see the end.
The chief priests and the whole council sought false witness against Jesus so they might put him to death,
but they found none, though many false witnesses came. Later two came forward
and said, This one said, I am able to destroy the temple of the Divine and through three days rebuild it.
The high priest stood and said to him, Do you answer nothing? What are these testifying against you?
But Jesus was silent. The high priest said, I adjure you by the living Divine: tell us whether you are the Messiah, the Son of the Divine.
Jesus said to him, You have said it. Yet I say to you: from now you will see the Son of Humanity sitting at the right hand of Power and coming on the clouds of heaven.
Then the high priest tore his garments, saying, He blasphemed. What further need do we have of witnesses? Look, now you have heard the blasphemy.
What do you think? They answered, He is liable to death.
Then they spat in his face and struck him; others slapped him,
saying, Prophesy to us, Messiah. Who struck you?
Peter was sitting outside in the courtyard, and one servant girl came to him, saying, You also were with Jesus the Galilean.
But he denied it before all, saying, I do not know what you mean.
When he went out to the gate, another saw him and said to those there, This one was with Jesus the Nazarene.
Again he denied with an oath, I do not know the man.
After a little while, those standing came and said to Peter, Truly you also are one of them, for even your speech makes you clear.
Then he began to curse and swear, I do not know the man. Immediately a rooster sounded.
Peter remembered the word Jesus had spoken, Before a rooster sounds, you will deny me three times. Going outside, he wept bitterly.
